en: Caretaker
Witch
Blacksmith
You
You came back. I'd hoped...well. A year is a long time to hope for something.
What happened to the core? It's gone.
One night the cracks just... gave way. We don't know how or why. We just know it's gone.
But you're here now, and that matters more than the why. You should speak to the Blacksmith and the Witch. They'll want to know you've come back.
You've got some nerve, showing up now. You've been gone for an entire year.
...I came as soon as I heard.
Sure you did. Fine. You're here now, that's what counts. I'm not much for talking. If you want to help, get to work.
Ah. You. I wondered when the town would pull you back.
You knew I was coming?
I know things are wrong. I've known since the night it happened, there's something in the residue of it. It didn't feel like an accident.
But I can't say more than that. Not yet.
Good. They're still standing. That's better than I feared on some nights.
The Witch thinks it wasn't an accident.
She's not wrong to feel that way. The Undoing doesn't shatter cores, it corrupts them, slowly. Someone or something else must have finished it.
But we rebuild with work, not grief. You'll need this to collect some wood from the trees around town. Just approach one and press the use tool button.
Let's start with the Town Hall, it was once the heart of this place after all. Let's make it the heart again. We'll need 10 wood to begin with.
There. It's no longer what it was, but at least it's standing.
Do you know how long I've been waiting to repair it, wondering if anyone would come to help?
You could have started without me.
No. Some things need to be done together, or they don't mean anything. I've learned that the hard way, more than once.
The Blacksmith's forge is next. She won't ask for help, though. You'll have to offer it.
You'll need some ore to relight the forge. Take this pickaxe to mine for those.
Just approach an ore and press the use tool button, your tool with auto-swap to the correct one.
Speak to the Blacksmith before gathering the ore, she'll let you know exactly what she needs.
This wood will help fix the cracks in the roof, perfect. We'll need a lot more before it's truly whole.
The Town Hall looks much better. I understand that was your doing?
You want to fix the forge as well. Fine. I won't pretend I don't need the help.
That's one way to ask for help. So then, what do you need?
Wood for the frame. Coal for the fuel. Iron for the anvil and the fittings.
Good timber. Dry enough. Keep going.
This'll do. I should get a decent fire going from this coal.
That's the last of it. I can fix the anvil with this iron.
The forge is lit. First time since... well. For the first time in awhile is all I'll say.
You know, I thought about leaving after it happened. Didn't see the point of a forge with no town. Glad I didn't.
Anyways, take this sword. It's not much, we can talk later about something better, but you'll need it if you're going to venture into the forest.
What's happened to the forest?
Speak with the Witch. She'll tell you about the dangers in the forest, but first she needs help fixing her Alchemy shop.
I've been piecing it back together in my head for months. The ingredients I lost, the shelves, the light from that particular window.
You can't replace everything, but maybe you can make it liveable again.
If you want to help, I'll need some wood for the shelves, stone for a new mortar and pestle, and...something special from the forest.
The Blacksmith mentioned the forest and that you would explain why it's suddenly dangerous.
Since we lost the core, monsters have started appearing outside the town. The sword the Blacksmith gave you should keep you safe enough.
I will need 3 green slime cubes from the green slimes in the forest. They are fairly weak, I'm sure you'll manage just fine.
This wood will make for a nice shelf.
Yes, this stone will make a fine mortar and pestle.
These slimes will do nicely. Don't ask questions for now, come back to me later and we'll discuss potions.
It's not finished, and likely won't be for a long time. But it's enough to allow me to work again.
We should discuss next steps in the core investigation.
Do you have a theory yet?
I do not believe that the core failed on its own. It was ended. Someone stood in the keeper's circle and made a choice.
What you must understand is that type of knowledge is rare. Most people in this town are unaware of the mystical properties of the core. Most people.
Every stone we lay is a promise. I have to believe that.
Work to do. Always more work to do.
The town is quieter than it should be. I'm still listening.
One more thing before you go investigating anything. You'll need a crafting table if you're going to be making potions for me.
I have a spare. Here, take it. Find somewhere sensible to put it in town. You'll learn the recipe to craft one yourself soon enough.
There's a decoration mode to place furniture. Hit Esc to see the decoration mode button. You can arrange things however you like.
I once rearranged my entire workshop at three in the morning. Very therapeutic.
Ah that's a perfect place for it. You know you can craft from your inventory and chests?
There's nothing worse than realising you forgot to pick up the materials you need. Have fun crafting!
